reasoning: This is a desirable property. `container_subtree_set_wf` requires that if `sub_c_ptr` is in `c_ptr`'s subtree, then `sub_c_ptr`'s depth > `c_ptr`'s depth. Since depth is a `usize` (non-negative), a container cannot have depth strictly greater than itself. The spec correctly prevents self-inclusion in subtrees.

The ensures clause `!container_perms.dom().contains(p_ptr) || container_perms.dom().contains(p_ptr)` is a tautology (law of excluded middle). It proves nothing about the spec — it would verify with any preconditions. The underlying observation about missing owned_procs constraints may be valid, but this PHI doesn't demonstrate it.

The ensures is `true` which is trivially satisfied, but the real test is precondition satisfiability. Neither `container_perms_wf` nor `container_tree_wf` constrains `owned_endpoints` in any way — the same endpoint can appear in multiple containers' `owned_endpoints` sets. Given the code comment explicitly mentions endpoint ownership semantics ("each endpoint can ONLY be referenced by threads in the subtree"), this missing disjointness constraint is a real spec gap.

This confirms that `container_perms_wf` works correctly — it explicitly includes the clause `container_perms[c_ptr].value().children@.contains(c_ptr) == false`. The spec intentionally prevents self-containment, and this PHI verifies that design is in place.
